mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 60% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses 4.5%, labor market tightness triggers wage inflation fears and prompts aggressive Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production indicates resilient corporate output that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes during tightening cycles produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, consistently predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 0.4% monthly, markets price in imminent aggressive rate hikes and margin compression, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal consumption expenditures grow faster than real GDP indicates consumer-driven economic expansion, reliably producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP growth spread fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, declining industrial production signals lingering supply chain bottlenecks and weak corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during recovery regimes yield positive forward returns in more than 60% of cases.
